title: CPI Sector Rotation
description: CPI print triggers massive sector rotation. Money moves out of mega-cap tech into small caps and value. Russell 2000 rips

title: Cliff Kicker — NFP Was Stinky
description: August NFP came in weak, triggering recession fears. First 'cliff kicker' — market drops off a ledge

title: Yen Flash Crash
description: Japanese yen carry trade unwind triggers global flash crash. NQ drops ~1,000+ pts. Yen spikes as leveraged positions liquidate

title: BLS Deletes 818K Jobs in Annual Revision
description: Bureau of Labor Statistics revises payrolls down by 818K in annual benchmark revision. Labor market was much weaker than reported

title: Cliff Kicker — Weak PMI
description: ISM Manufacturing PMI comes in weak. Second cliff kicker event — market drops sharply on growth fears

title: September NFP Miss
description: September NFP disappoints. Reinforces the weak labor market narrative post-BLS revision

title: Fed's First Cut — 50bps
description: Federal Reserve cuts rates by 50bps in first cut of the cycle. Larger than expected. Market rallies on dovish surprise

title: A Patriot's Cliche
description: Post-rate-cut continuation rally. Market pricing in soft landing narrative

title: ISI Tensions Reflare
description: Israel-Iran tensions reflare in early October. Geopolitical risk premium spikes. Oil and defense names move

title: People Bet on Trump — War Tactics
description: Polymarket and prediction markets show surging Trump odds. 'War tactics' positioning — defense, energy, financials bid

title: Election 2024 — Trump II
description: Donald Trump wins 2024 presidential election. Markets rip higher on 'Trump Trade' — financials, energy, small caps lead. Mega-cap tech mixed

title: Fed Pivot + Election Chaos
description: The Fed actually pivoted here but the election overshadowed it. Trump election win triggered 'Trump Trade' frenzy

title: CPI Print +/-
description: November CPI release — mixed data, market digesting post-election macro picture

title: Strong Data — Trump Trade Frenzy
description: More strong economic data fueling the post-election Trump trade momentum. Markets ripping higher

title: Inflation Under Control (?)
description: Data suggested inflation was under control heading into December FOMC, but uncertainty lingered

title: FOMC Solidifies Hawkish Pivot
description: December FOMC meeting: Fed cut 25bps but dot plot showed fewer cuts ahead. Hawkish pivot solidified. Markets sold off hard

title: 1Y Candle Closure Pullback
description: Year-end rebalancing and annual candle closure causing technical pullback. Typical holiday thin liquidity

title: Silent Issue: 2Y/10Y Spread, JPY, Tariffs
description: 2Y/10Y yield spread inversion concerns, yen strengthening, early tariff rhetoric creating a toxic cocktail under the surface

title: Strong NFP Beat — Less Cuts?
description: January NFP came in strong. Markets repricing fewer rate cuts in 2025. Good-news-is-bad-news dynamic

title: CPI Revives Sentiment
description: January CPI print came in softer than expected, reviving risk-on sentiment and reversing the NFP-driven selloff

title: Strong Earnings — CES '25
description: Strong earnings season kicks off, coinciding with CES 2025 tech showcase. AI narrative still dominant

title: DeepSeek Rattles Big Tech
description: Chinese AI startup DeepSeek releases model rivaling GPT-4 at fraction of cost. Nasdaq drops 3%. AI capex narrative questioned

title: CA & MX Tariffs Announced
description: Trump announces 25% tariffs on Canada and Mexico, 10% on China. Markets rattle on trade war fears

title: February NFP Beat
description: NFP beat expectations again, reinforcing the 'higher for longer' rate narrative

title: Plunge Patrol: Bonds Oversold During Super Bowl
description: Bond market oversold during Super Bowl weekend. Thin liquidity + positioning created extreme moves

title: Reciprocal Tariffs on China
description: Trump imposes 25% tariffs on steel and aluminum from China. Escalation from initial 10% levy

title: First Hot CPI in a While — Trump Trade Value Buy
description: CPI came in hot but market treated it as a dip-buying opportunity. Trump trade rotation into value continued

title: Hostages Killed / Bad WMT / Dimon & Karp Off / Claims Sticky
description: Hamas kills hostages. Walmart earnings disappoint. JPMorgan's Dimon and Palantir's Karp sell shares. Jobless claims sticky

title: Trump Budges on EU Tariffs + Chip Contracts
description: Trump softens stance on EU tariffs and signals chip export contract flexibility. Partial trade war de-escalation

title: NVDA Earnings
description: Nvidia earnings report — key bellwether for AI capex narrative post-DeepSeek scare

title: Trump Calls for 25% Tariffs on EU
description: Trump escalates EU trade tensions with call for 25% tariffs. European markets sell off

title: Crypto Summit / Fed Signals / Tariff Rollback
description: White House crypto summit sends positive signals. Fed signals flexibility. Some tariff rollback announced

title: Markets Fully Price In Tariffs / Yen to Flash Crash Levels
description: Markets fully pricing in tariff impact. Japanese yen strengthens to levels seen during the August flash crash

title: Good CPI — Rejection
description: CPI came in good (soft) but market rejected the move higher. Tariff uncertainty overriding macro data

title: Good PPI — Rejection
description: PPI came in soft but market again rejected. Tariff narrative dominating all macro prints

title: Dovish Fed — Tariffs Are Transitory?
description: FOMC meeting: Fed takes dovish stance, signals tariff inflation impact is transitory. Market briefly rallies

title: Trump: Leniency on Some Tariffs
description: Trump signals leniency on select tariffs. Partial de-escalation creates short-lived relief rally

title: LIBERATION DAY
description: Trump declares 'Liberation Day' with sweeping reciprocal tariffs — 10% baseline on all countries, 60+ countries hit with higher rates. Market crashes

title: 90-Day Tariff Pause — 'Now's a Good Time to Buy'
description: Trump pauses reciprocal tariffs for 90 days (except China at 125%). Market rips 10%+ in historic rally. 'Now is a good time to buy'

title: Bessent: Handling Tariff Rhetoric / Trump Wants to Fire Powell
description: Treasury Secretary Bessent manages tariff messaging. Trump publicly states desire to fire Fed Chair Powell. Market rattled

title: Trump: 'Never Wanted to Fire Powell, Discussing China Deal'
description: Trump walks back Powell firing rhetoric. Signals deal discussions with China. Market recovers

title: Bessent: Reverting to Phase 1 China / Bad Jobs Data
description: Bessent signals reversion to Phase 1 framework for China trade talks. Trump stays quiet on Powell. Jobs data disappoints

title: NFP Sticks the Landing / S&P500 Historic Streak
description: May NFP lands right on expectations. S&P 500 notches historic winning streak. Goldilocks vibes return

title: Trump & Putin Fake the Funk / Calm CPI / Peace Treaty
description: 3rd negative signal. Trump-Putin peace negotiations theatrics. CPI comes in calm. Markets digest mixed signals

title: Say It With Me: China.
description: China trade narrative re-emerges as dominant market driver. Everything revolves around US-China relations

title: Fitch Downgrades Credit / Bessent: Disregard / Bonds: Hold My Beer
description: Fitch downgrades US credit. Bessent says disregard it. Bond market disagrees violently. Yields spike

title: Strong NFP
description: June NFP comes in strong. Labor market remains resilient despite trade war uncertainty

title: Operation Midnight Hammer
description: Hold my beer moment — overnight market structure event. Dramatic move in thin liquidity

title: MSFT / CrowdStrike Cybershock
description: Microsoft and CrowdStrike involved in major cybersecurity incident. Tech sector rattled

title: EU Trade Deal, 15% Tariffs / US-China Round 2 Begins
description: EU trade deal reached at 15% tariff rate. US-China round 2 trade negotiations begin. Mixed signals

title: Trump: 50% on China / Aug 16 Deadline / Powell: Not Hiking
description: Trump threatens 50% tariffs on China with Aug 16 deadline. Powell says Fed is 'looking through inflation' and NOT hiking rates

title: But AMZN, MSFT.
description: Amazon and Microsoft earnings provide counter-narrative to doom. Big tech still delivering

title: NFP Revisions -244K / Rate Traders Pile Into Oct Cut Bets
description: NFP revised down by 244K jobs. Interest rate traders aggressively adding October rate cut bets. Labor market weaker than thought
